select_when: Early choices are constraining current options more than people realize, so history matters more than present-day preference alone.
A system keeps reproducing old behavior despite new intent because inherited grooves are stronger than the latest goals.
Changing course requires more than a better argument because habits, standards, interfaces, or prior investments are locking the system into its route.
A migration or redesign looks clean on paper, but installed constraints are already narrowing the real path out.
avoid_when: Path-lock is being diagnosed so broadly that it becomes an excuse for not redesigning anything that could actually change.
The model is being used to romanticize history when the live issue is really current economics, current incentives, or an untested assumption.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on inherited constraint, route narrowing, and redesign difficulty rather than on broad historical narrative.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor migration-policy sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on inherited architecture, standards, and route-lock effects.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The second avoid-when line is reviewed normalization over a source that is stronger on anti-stagnation than on explicit anti-activation routing.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that path dependence is strongest when inherited architecture, standards, or prior commitments narrow current options.

avoid_when.note: The anti-excuse boundary is source-supported; the second anti-activation line is conservative reviewed normalization.

input_type.note: The raw source activates on inherited-route and redesign-friction problems, but the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields a clearer frame for historical constraint and switching difficulty; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Path dependence clearly relies on counterfactual reversal thinking and system-route analysis, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
